Personal style system

Alt Style Guide

A hybrid fashion magazine and wardrobe planner: themes pulled from your outfit references, with practical buy-first priorities and shopping links.

Band Tee UniformRomantic Goth SkirtsPunk Print & ColourSoft Corporate NoirSleek Evening & MetallicsDark Academia PrepShopping

Buy first

Black wide-leg denim, one great blazer, one long black skirt, chunky black shoes, and a chain belt. These unlock the most outfits.

Then add drama

Mesh, lace, faux leather, red plaid, leopard, and metallic pleats. One statement piece at a time keeps it wearable.

Finish with hardware

Layered necklaces, crossbody bags, pointed shoes, Mary Janes, wallet chains, and silver details make basics feel styled.

Theme 01

Band Tee Uniform

The daily anchor

Graphic tee, black denim, metal hardware, and a shoe with attitude. This is the base layer that makes the rest of the wardrobe feel like you.

Outfit formula: Band tee + high-rise denim + chain detail + one intentional shoe. Add a blazer, cardigan, or leather jacket when you want it to feel more styled.

Prioritise buying

  • A black or charcoal wide-leg jean
  • Two oversized band tees, one black and one faded grey
  • Chain belt or wallet chain
  • Vans, platform loafers, or chunky Mary Janes
  • A black crossbody that does not feel too polished
Theme 02

Romantic Goth Skirts

Lace, movement, softness

The prettiest lane: mesh, lace, floaty black skirts, red florals, and heavy shoes so it never gets too sweet.

Outfit formula: Mesh or lace top + long skirt + platform shoe. Keep the palette black, burgundy, cream, or deep red.

Prioritise buying

  • Black lace or mesh long-sleeve top
  • Black midi or maxi skirt with lace or tiers
  • Red-and-black floral skirt
  • Platform Mary Janes or combat boots
  • Layered necklaces with one cross or pendant
Theme 03

Punk Print & Colour

The controlled chaos lane

Plaid, leopard, neon green, red accents, and leather. This is where the wardrobe gets funny, loud, and very you.

Outfit formula: One loud piece + black base + one matching accent. Leopard skirt with black tee, plaid trousers with black cardigan, neon bag with band tee.

Prioritise buying

  • One red or green plaid trouser
  • Leopard or cheetah midi skirt
  • A red cardigan or red bag
  • Black leather jacket
  • Low-top sneakers and chunky boots
Theme 04

Soft Corporate Noir

Office, but make it moody

Blazers, trench coats, wide trousers, sharp shoes, and black layers. Polished without becoming glossy or bland.

Outfit formula: Black base + structured outer layer + one polished shoe. Add red, burgundy, grey, or cream to stop it feeling flat.

Prioritise buying

  • Black relaxed blazer
  • Cream or stone trench coat
  • Black wide-leg trousers
  • Grey pleated or plaid trouser
  • Pointed flats or low heels in black, red, or burgundy
Theme 05

Sleek Evening & Metallics

Dinner, date, gallery, party

A quieter, more grown-up dramatic lane: satin, faux leather, metallic pleats, pointed shoes, and stronger jewellery.

Outfit formula: Sleek skirt or trouser + simple knit or mesh layer + pointed shoe. This is the high-impact evening capsule.

Prioritise buying

  • Black satin bias skirt
  • Faux leather midi skirt or trousers
  • Metallic pleated skirt
  • Pointed Mary Jane heels or pointed flats
  • Small structured bag
Theme 06

Dark Academia Prep

Sharper layers

White shirts, sweater vests, argyle, plaid, grey, black, loafers, and long coats. It gives the goth wardrobe a bookish spine.

Outfit formula: White shirt + black vest or blazer + plaid/grey bottom + black shoe. Add a chain or pendant to keep it from going too classic.

Prioritise buying

  • Oversized white button-down
  • Black sweater vest
  • Grey or black plaid trousers/skirt
  • Black loafers or Mary Janes
  • Long black coat
